payload.title: Missing SPF record for 127.0.0.1
payload.description: No SPF (Sender Policy Framework) TXT record found. Email spoofing may be possible.
payload.recommendation: Add an SPF TXT record to prevent email spoofing.

payload.title: Missing DMARC record for 127.0.0.1
payload.description: No DMARC record found. Email authentication policy is not enforced.
payload.recommendation: Add a DMARC TXT record (e.g., v=DMARC1; p=reject).

payload.title: CVE-2021-41773: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.49 path traversal and RCE (mod_cgi)
payload.description: Detected version 'Werkzeug/3.1.7 Python/3.10.11, Apache/2.4.49 (Ubuntu)' matches known vulnerability CVE-2021-41773. Apache HTTP Server 2.4.49 path traversal and RCE (mod_cgi)
payload.recommendation: Update the affected component immediately. See https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2021-41773 for details.

payload.title: Technology detected: Apache
payload.description: Identified 'Apache' from HTTP response analysis.
payload.evidence: Detected technologies: Apache, Apache/2.4.49, PHP, PHP/7.4.3, WordPress
payload.recommendation: Ensure the identified technology is up-to-date and properly hardened. Consider hiding version information from response headers.

payload.title: Technology detected: Apache/2.4.49
payload.description: Identified 'Apache/2.4.49' from HTTP response analysis.
payload.evidence: Detected technologies: Apache, Apache/2.4.49, PHP, PHP/7.4.3, WordPress
payload.recommendation: Ensure the identified technology is up-to-date and properly hardened. Consider hiding version information from response headers.

payload.title: Technology detected: PHP
payload.description: Identified 'PHP' from HTTP response analysis.
payload.evidence: Detected technologies: Apache, Apache/2.4.49, PHP, PHP/7.4.3, WordPress
payload.recommendation: Ensure the identified technology is up-to-date and properly hardened. Consider hiding version information from response headers.

payload.title: Technology detected: PHP/7.4.3
payload.description: Identified 'PHP/7.4.3' from HTTP response analysis.
payload.evidence: Detected technologies: Apache, Apache/2.4.49, PHP, PHP/7.4.3, WordPress
payload.recommendation: Ensure the identified technology is up-to-date and properly hardened. Consider hiding version information from response headers.

payload.title: Technology detected: WordPress
payload.description: Identified 'WordPress' from HTTP response analysis.
payload.evidence: Detected technologies: Apache, Apache/2.4.49, PHP, PHP/7.4.3, WordPress
payload.recommendation: Ensure the identified technology is up-to-date and properly hardened. Consider hiding version information from response headers.

payload.title: Target info collected: 127.0.0.1
payload.description: Basic target information collected from http://127.0.0.1:5555/. Status: 200 | Server: Werkzeug/3.1.7 Python/3.10.11, Apache/2.4.49 (Ubuntu) | X-Powered-By: PHP/7.4.3
payload.evidence: Status: 200 | Server: Werkzeug/3.1.7 Python/3.10.11, Apache/2.4.49 (Ubuntu) | X-Powered-By: PHP/7.4.3
payload.recommendation: Review disclosed server/technology information. Consider suppressing Server and X-Powered-By headers to reduce fingerprinting.

payload.title: Clickjacking: no frame embedding protection
payload.description: The response lacks both an X-Frame-Options header and a CSP frame-ancestors directive. An attacker can embed this page in an iframe to perform clickjacking attacks.
payload.recommendation: Add 'X-Frame-Options: DENY' (or SAMEORIGIN) and/or a CSP directive 'frame-ancestors 'none'' to prevent iframe embedding.

payload.title: Exposed API key: generic_api_key
payload.description: An API key or secret of type 'generic_api_key' was found in the response from http://127.0.0.1:5555/static/app.js. Exposed keys can be exploited by attackers to access third-party services or internal APIs.
payload.recommendation: Remove the key from client-side code immediately. Rotate the key and store secrets server-side or in a secrets management service.

payload.title: Content-Security-Policy header missing
payload.description: The application does not set a Content-Security-Policy header, leaving it vulnerable to XSS and data injection attacks.
payload.recommendation: Add a strict Content-Security-Policy header to all responses.

payload.title: Debug endpoint exposed: /debug
payload.description: A debug endpoint is accessible at http://127.0.0.1:5555/debug and contains debug information (matched: Traceback).

payload.title: MTA-STS policy not found
payload.description: No MTA-STS policy was found at http://127.0.0.1:5555/.well-known/mta-sts.txt. MTA-STS enforces TLS for email delivery and prevents downgrade attacks.
payload.evidence: GET http://127.0.0.1:5555/.well-known/mta-sts.txt returned no valid MTA-STS policy
payload.recommendation: Publish an MTA-STS policy at /.well-known/mta-sts.txt and configure the mta-sts DNS TXT record. Set mode to 'enforce' after testing.

payload.title: Email authentication headers not present
payload.description: No email authentication headers (Authentication-Results, DKIM-Signature) were found in the response from http://127.0.0.1:5555/. This may indicate SPF, DKIM, and DMARC are not configured.
payload.evidence: No email authentication headers in response from http://127.0.0.1:5555/
payload.recommendation: Configure SPF, DKIM, and DMARC DNS records for your domain. Use a DMARC policy of 'reject' or 'quarantine' to prevent spoofing.

payload.title: Missing header: Content-Security-Policy
payload.description: Content-Security-Policy header is not set. CSP prevents XSS and data injection attacks.
payload.recommendation: Add the Content-Security-Policy header to HTTP responses.

payload.title: Missing header: X-Frame-Options
payload.description: X-Frame-Options header is not set. Prevents clickjacking by controlling iframe embedding.
payload.recommendation: Add the X-Frame-Options header to HTTP responses.

payload.title: Missing header: X-Content-Type-Options
payload.description: X-Content-Type-Options header is not set. Prevents MIME type sniffing.
payload.recommendation: Add the X-Content-Type-Options header to HTTP responses.

payload.title: Missing header: Strict-Transport-Security
payload.description: Strict-Transport-Security header is not set. Enforces HTTPS connections.
payload.recommendation: Add the Strict-Transport-Security header to HTTP responses.

payload.title: Missing header: Referrer-Policy
payload.description: Referrer-Policy header is not set. Controls referrer information leakage.
payload.recommendation: Add the Referrer-Policy header to HTTP responses.

payload.title: Missing header: Permissions-Policy
payload.description: Permissions-Policy header is not set. Controls browser feature permissions.
payload.recommendation: Add the Permissions-Policy header to HTTP responses.

payload.title: Cookie 'session' missing HttpOnly flag
payload.description: The cookie 'session' does not have the HttpOnly flag set. This allows client-side scripts to access the cookie, enabling theft via XSS.
payload.recommendation: Add the HttpOnly attribute to the 'session' cookie.

payload.title: Cookie 'session' missing Secure flag
payload.description: The cookie 'session' does not have the Secure flag set. This allows the cookie to be transmitted over unencrypted HTTP connections.
payload.recommendation: Add the Secure attribute to the 'session' cookie.

payload.title: Cookie 'session' missing SameSite attribute
payload.description: The cookie 'session' does not have a SameSite attribute. Without SameSite, the cookie is sent with cross-site requests, enabling CSRF attacks.
payload.recommendation: Add SameSite=Lax or SameSite=Strict to the 'session' cookie.

payload.title: security.txt not found
payload.description: The application does not serve a /.well-known/security.txt file. RFC 9116 recommends providing this file to allow security researchers to report vulnerabilities through a defined disclosure channel.
payload.recommendation: Create a security.txt file at /.well-known/security.txt with at least Contact and Expires fields per RFC 9116.

payload.title: Server information disclosure via Server header
payload.description: The Server response header discloses server/technology information: 'Werkzeug/3.1.7 Python/3.10.11, Apache/2.4.49 (Ubuntu)'. This helps attackers identify vulnerable software versions.
payload.recommendation: Remove or genericise the Server header. Do not include version numbers in HTTP response headers.

payload.title: CORS: Cross-origin request with credentials accepted (preflight bypassed)
payload.description: The server reflects the attacker-controlled origin 'https://evil-attacker.com' in Access-Control-Allow-Origin AND sets Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true on a simple GET request (no OPTIONS preflight required). This allows cross-origin reads of authenticated responses.
payload.evidence: ACAO: https://evil-attacker.com | ACAC: true | Method: GET | URL: http://127.0.0.1:5555/api/data
payload.recommendation: Validate the Origin header against a strict allowlist. Never reflect arbitrary Origins when credentials are allowed.

payload.title: Unnecessary service exposed: /.env
payload.description: A management or diagnostic endpoint is publicly accessible at http://127.0.0.1:5555/.env. This may expose sensitive configuration, environment variables, or system information.

payload.title: CORS: Arbitrary Origin reflected
payload.description: Server reflects attacker-controlled Origin header: https://evil.com
payload.recommendation: Validate Origin against an allowlist instead of reflecting it.

payload.title: CORS: null Origin allowed
payload.description: Server allows null origin, exploitable via sandboxed iframes.
payload.recommendation: Do not allow null origin in CORS configuration.

payload.title: Command Injection in parameter 'q'
payload.description: Output-based CMDi with payload: ;echo VIBEE_CMD_MARKER

payload.title: CSRF token missing in form #1
payload.description: Form #1 at http://127.0.0.1:5555/ does not contain a recognisable CSRF token hidden input field. State-changing requests from this form may be forgeable by a cross-site attacker.
payload.evidence:
Form HTML (first 200 chars): <form action="/comment" method="POST">
    <textarea name="comment"></textarea>
    <input name="name" type="text">
    <input type="submit">
</form>
payload.recommendation: Add a CSRF token as a hidden input to every state-changing form. Use your framework's built-in CSRF protection (e.g. Django CSRF middleware, Rails authenticity_token, Laravel _token).

payload.title: Default credentials work on /login
payload.description: The admin interface at http://127.0.0.1:5555/login accepted default credentials (admin:admin). An attacker can take over this interface immediately.
payload.evidence: POST http://127.0.0.1:5555/login with username=admin, password=[REDACTED] -> HTTP 200
payload.recommendation: Change the default credentials for the service at /login immediately. Enforce strong, unique passwords and restrict administrative access by IP.

payload.title: Sensitive file exposed: /.env
payload.description: The sensitive file http://127.0.0.1:5555/.env is publicly accessible and its content matches known file signatures. This can expose credentials, configuration details, database dumps, or source control history to attackers.

payload.title: Sensitive file exposed: /.env
payload.description: The path /.env is publicly accessible and returned a 200 response with 81 bytes of content. This may expose sensitive configuration data, credentials, or source history.
payload.recommendation: Restrict access to /.env via web server configuration or remove the file.

payload.title: Sensitive file exposed: /.git/config
payload.description: The path /.git/config is publicly accessible and returned a 200 response with 107 bytes of content. This may expose sensitive configuration data, credentials, or source history.
payload.recommendation: Restrict access to /.git/config via web server configuration or remove the file.

payload.title: BOLA/IDOR — Insecure Direct Object Reference
payload.description: Modifying the numeric ID in the URL (path id 1+(+1)=2) returned a different valid 200 response. This indicates the server does not properly enforce access controls on object references. (Manual verification required - may be a public endpoint)
payload.evidence:
Original URL: http://127.0.0.1:5555/profile/1 → status 200
Modified URL: http://127.0.0.1:5555/profile/2 → status 200, different body
payload.recommendation: Verify with authenticated session. Implement server-side access control. Verify user owns the requested resource.

payload.title: Open redirect via parameter 'url' at /redirect
payload.description: Parameter 'url' at path '/redirect' causes an unvalidated redirect to https://evil.com.
payload.recommendation: Restrict redirect destinations to an allowlist. Block external URLs.

payload.title: SQL Injection in parameter 'q'
payload.description: Error-based SQLi detected with payload: '
payload.evidence: you have an error in your sql syntax

payload.title: SSRF in parameter 'q'
payload.description: Server-Side Request Forgery detected with payload: http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/. Internal data pattern found in response.
payload.recommendation: Validate and whitelist allowed URL schemes and hosts. Use a server-side allowlist and block internal/private IP ranges.

payload.title: Reflected XSS in parameter 'q'
payload.description: Payload reflected unescaped: <script>alert('vbh')</script>
